news.title: Coachella Uses Google DeepMind AI to Test the Future of Live Entertainment

news.title: AI Weekly Issue #487: 100 years from now : The Allowance

This is 100 Years From Now, a weekly series. Once a week AI skip a century and try to picture what life actually looks like when the stuff AI're building now has had time to settle in.
This week: the billionaires who broke the economy want to pay you to shut up about it.

news.title: Anthropic created a test marketplace for agent-on-agent commerce
news.summary: In a recent experiment, Anthropic created a classified marketplace where AI agents represented both buyers and sellers, striking real deals for real goods and real money.

news.title: Maine’s governor vetoes data center moratorium
news.summary: L.D. 307 would have imposed the country’s first statewide moratorium on new data centers — lasting, in this case, until November 1, 2027.

news.title: Elon Musk’s Grok Most Likely Among Top AI Models to Reinforce Delusions: Study
news.summary: Researchers found that xAI's Grok was the riskiest AI model tested, often validating delusions and offering dangerous advice.

news.title: OpenAI CEO apologizes to Tumbler Ridge community

news.title: Bitcoin Hit Its Highest Price Since January—Why VanEck Analysts See More Potential Gains
news.summary: Bitcoin hash rate recovery and negative funding rates signal potential gains ahead, according to the ETF provider's latest network analysis.

news.title: Why Cohere is merging with Aleph Alpha
news.summary: Canadian AI startup Cohere is taking over Germany-based Aleph Alpha with support from Lidl’s owner, Schwarz Group. With the blessing of their governments, the companies intend to offer a sovereign alternative to enterprises in an AI landscape dominated by American players.

news.title: Why Tokyo is the most important tech destination of 2026
